text: India-US strategic partnership has strengthened significantly. Key frameworks: Quad (India, US, Japan, Australia), iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies), 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue. Defence agreements: LEMOA, COMCASA, BECA. Trade: US is India's largest trading partner ($190 billion, 2023-24). PM Modi visited US in June 2023 for State Dinner.

India-China relations remain complex. Boundary dispute along LAC led to 2020 Galwan clash (20 Indian soldiers killed). Disengagement ongoing at friction points. Trade: China is India's 2nd largest trading partner despite tensions ($136 billion, 2023-24). India banned 300+ Chinese apps. Bilateral issues: Tibet, Pakistan relationship, BRI opposition, UN Security Council seat.

India-Pakistan relations remain contentious. Key issues: Kashmir dispute, cross-border terrorism, Indus Water Treaty. Wars: 1947, 1965, 1971, Kargil 1999. Diplomatic ties downgraded after Article 370 abrogation (2019). Balakot airstrikes (Feb 2019) after Pulwama attack. No bilateral trade since 2019.

India and Russia share a 'Special and Privileged Strategic Partnership'. Russia is India's largest defence supplier (S-400, BrahMos, Sukhoi). India abstained from UN votes condemning Russia's Ukraine invasion. Bilateral trade increased significantly via Rupee-Rouble mechanism. PM Modi visited Moscow in July 2024.

BRICS is a group of major emerging economies. Original members: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa. Expanded in 2024 to include Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, UAE, Saudi Arabia. India hosted BRICS 2016 summit in Goa. BRICS established the New Development Bank (NDB) headquartered in Shanghai. India advocates for reformed multilateralism through BRICS.

India held the G20 Presidency from Dec 2022 to Nov 2023 with theme 'One Earth, One Family, One Future' (Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam). Key achievement: African Union admitted as permanent G20 member. New Delhi Declaration adopted by consensus including on Ukraine. Over 200 meetings held across 60 Indian cities. PM Modi called it 'people's G20'.
